response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior generalist with strong testing discipline, GIS exposure through GISCollective, and some C/C++ history at National Instruments — but that C++ work was a decade ago and brief. The role demands modern C++ (or Rust) as a primary skill plus navigation/routing pipeline experience, neither of which the resume demonstrates at the required depth. Recent work is firmly TypeScript/Node and DLang. Worth a screening call only if the team is open to ramping a strong generalist into C++.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS background via GISCollective and Green Map
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, low-latency systems at Sociomantic
Comfortable across stacks and languages, signals willingness to learn
concerns: No recent modern C++ or Rust experience; C/C++ stint was 2014–2015
No demonstrated work on routing tiles, map data pipelines, or navigation systems
Recent roles centered on TypeScript/Node, not systems-level performance code
